analysis.reasoning: Core message: A quote from Lukashevich states that ensuring full and equal participation of women in all spheres is a priority of Russian state policy. Evaluation by narrative: RU1 (West immoral/hostile) – Not referenced; no comparison or criticism of the West. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system) – Not explicit; while it hints at a values-based policy, it does not present an alternative order, multilateralism, or non-Western cooperation. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner) – Partially relevant in tone, but the tweet focuses on domestic policy, not partnership or external benevolence, so not applicable. RU4 (Russian history admirable) – Not about history. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront) – Implicitly portrays Russia as progressive on gender equality, which can serve as a soft-power “we lead/are advanced” signal, but the tweet does not claim leadership, superiority, or comparative success; it merely states a priority. Therefore RU5 is not clearly supported. RU6 (interesting culture/nature) – Not applicable. Conclusion: No listed narrative is directly promoted.
